---
title: "Argo OHC Sampling Bias — North Atlantic Current"
subtitle: "GP Modeling"
author: "Daniel Ross"
date: today
jupyter: python3
---

```{=html}
<style>
/* pandas/quarto dataframe tables inherit Bootstrap's .table (width:100%),
   which stretches them across the full page-layout container; size to
   content instead. */
table.dataframe { width: auto; max-width: 90%; font-size: 0.85em; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; }
</style>
```

```{python}
#| label: py-helpers
#| include: false

DEPTH_LABEL = {"ohc_700": "0–700 m", "ohc_2000": "0–2000 m"}
TABSET_FIGW, TABSET_FIGH = 8, 5

def _emit(md):
    print("\n" + md + "\n")

def _show_fig(fig):
    _emit('<div style="text-align:center">')
    display(fig)
    _emit("</div>")
    plt.close(fig)
```

# Setup

This report picks up where `nac.qmd`'s Analysis Region / OHC Anomaly sections leave off, without
re-running any of that report's plotting. The expensive step -- depth-integrating GLORYS `thetao`
over all 41 levels to get the truth OHC field -- is cached to `data/ohc_truth/truth_ohc_<year>.nc`
by `nac.qmd`; loading that cache plus the (cheap) RG climatology processing takes ~10s, vs. ~2.5 min
for `nac.qmd`'s full render (dominated by matplotlib/cartopy rendering of ~20 map panels, which
quarto has no way to skip short of freezing the whole file untouched).

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| code-summary: "Setup — imports and experiment configuration"
import sys, os
sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath("../code"))

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import xarray as xr
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

import ohc
import ohc_bias
import ohc_climatology as ohc_clim

YEARS = [2020, 2021, 2022]

VELOCITY_GLOB = {
    2020: "../data/velocity_gs_wide/velocity_2020_*.nc",
    2021: "../data/velocity_gs_2021_2022/velocity_2021_*.nc",
    2022: "../data/velocity_gs_2021_2022/velocity_2022_*.nc",
}
DOWNLOAD_BOUNDS = {
    2020: (30, 48, -74, -49),
    2021: (33, 45, -74, -49),
    2022: (33, 45, -74, -49),
}
ANALYSIS_BOUNDS = (36, 40, -68, -62)
OHC_TRUTH_DIR   = "../data/ohc_truth"
DEPTHS          = ["ohc_700", "ohc_2000"]
```

```{python}
#| label: load-cached-data
#| code-summary: "Load the cached truth OHC field and rebuild the RG climatology anomaly"
theta = {}
truth_field_full = {}
for y in YEARS:
    la0, la1, lo0, lo1 = DOWNLOAD_BOUNDS[y]
    theta[y] = xr.open_mfdataset(VELOCITY_GLOB[y]).sel(
        latitude=slice(la0, la1), longitude=slice(lo0, lo1),
    )
    truth_field_full[y] = xr.open_dataset(f"{OHC_TRUTH_DIR}/truth_ohc_{y}.nc").load()

mean_da = ohc_clim.load_rg_mean()
clim_field_B = ohc_clim.climatological_ohc_field(mean_da)  # static 2004-2018 mean

clim_field = {}
truth_field = {}
truth_anom = {}
truth_anom_cells = {}
for y in YEARS:
    ext_da = ohc_clim.load_rg_extensions(year=y)
    clim_field[y] = ohc_clim.climatological_ohc_field(mean_da + ext_da).compute()
    truth_field[y] = truth_field_full[y].sel(
        latitude=slice(ANALYSIS_BOUNDS[0], ANALYSIS_BOUNDS[1]),
        longitude=slice(ANALYSIS_BOUNDS[2], ANALYSIS_BOUNDS[3]),
    )
    truth_anom[y] = ohc_clim.truth_ohc_anomaly(truth_field[y], clim_field[y])
    truth_anom_cells[y] = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(truth_anom[y])

truth_anom_cells_all = pd.concat(truth_anom_cells.values(), ignore_index=True)
```

```{python}
#| label: real-argo
#| code-summary: "Load real Argo profiles in the analysis region, 2020-2022"
argo_all = pd.read_csv("../data/argo_ohc.csv")
argo_all["date"] = pd.to_datetime(argo_all["date"], format="ISO8601")
la0, la1, lo0, lo1 = ANALYSIS_BOUNDS
argo_a = argo_all[
    argo_all.lat.between(la0, la1)
    & argo_all.lon.between(lo0, lo1)
    & argo_all["date"].dt.year.isin(YEARS)
]
len(argo_a)
```

# GP Modeling

*TODO*
